message:
fix: defer useHour state updates to prevent React hydration mismatch

Both header-icon-3d.tsx and three-scene.tsx contain useHour hooks that
determine time-based animations and color palettes. Previously, these
hooks called setHour(new Date().getHours()) synchronously within
useEffect, which caused the first client render to differ from the
server-rendered HTML (which defaults to 12/noon).

This mismatch triggered React hydration warnings because the server
HTML showed one hour but the client immediately rendered with the
actual current hour before React could reconcile the DOM.

The fix uses setTimeout(update, 0) to push the actual hour update to
the next event loop tick, ensuring:
1. First client render matches the server-rendered default (hour 12)
2. Hydration completes successfully with matching content
3. State then updates to actual hour after hydration is done

Both files also now properly clean up the hydration timeout in the
useEffect cleanup function to prevent memory leaks if the component
unmounts before the timeout fires.

This follows the same pattern used in the previous hydration fix
(commit b8583cb) for date initialization issues.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
